assertions.title: Responsive at 768px viewport

steps.observed: Worldview section loaded successfully with scene strip and content

steps.action: Create scene and seed store with world + 4 takes
steps.expected: World card and takes grid visible
steps.observed: World card displayed with 600x400 placeholder, 4 takes in 3-column grid

steps.expected: Layout renders correctly, no horizontal overflow
steps.observed: With 1 scene: layout renders correctly, no overflow. Sidebar icons hidden. Takes grid remains 3 columns. No horizontal overflow.

steps.action: Add 6 more scenes (total 7) to test scene strip scroll at 768px
steps.expected: Scene strip scrolls horizontally, no page-level horizontal overflow
steps.observed: Scene strip has overflow-x:auto and scrolls horizontally (scrollWidth 1026 > clientWidth 997). However, page-level horizontal overflow occurs (bodyScrollWidth 1045 > bodyClientWidth 768). Root cause: .flex-1 main content container lacks min-w-0, so it expands to content width.

steps.action: Check horizontal overflow with DOM inspection at 768px
steps.observed: With multiple scenes: bodyScrollWidth=1045 vs bodyClientWidth=768 — horizontal overflow present. The .flex-1 element (main content) is 1021px wide because it lacks min-width:0.

steps.action: Set viewport to 639px (below 640px) to test 2-column takes grid
steps.observed: Takes grid correctly switches to 2 columns (grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 — sm breakpoint at 640px). Grid template columns: 504.656px 504.656px.
Horizontal overflow present: bodyScrollWidth=1053 vs bodyClientWidth=639. Same root cause — .flex-1 container lacks min-w-0.

evidence.network: N/A — no network requests for this assertion

assertions.issues: Horizontal overflow occurs at narrow viewports (768px and below) when content is wider than viewport. The .flex-1 main content container in KanvasPage layout lacks min-w-0 (Tailwind min-w-0), causing it to expand beyond its flex parent. Scene strip overflow-x-auto works for its own content but cannot contain the parent overflow. Takes grid correctly uses grid-cols-2 below sm:640px breakpoint. Fix: add min-w-0 class to the .flex-1 element in the main content layout.

steps.action: Inspect all buttons in worldview section for aria-label/title attributes
steps.expected: All interactive elements have proper aria-label or title
steps.observed: All 20 interactive elements within the worldview section have proper accessibility attributes. Scene chips: aria-label='Scene N' + aria-selected. Remove buttons: aria-label='Remove Scene N'. New Scene: aria-label='New Scene'. Take refresh buttons: aria-label='Refresh take'. Prompt input: aria-label='Prompt'.

steps.action: Check scene chips for aria-selected attribute
steps.observed: All scene chips (role='button') have aria-selected attribute. Active scene has aria-selected='true', others have aria-selected='false'.

steps.action: Enter world-viewer mode and inspect HUD elements
steps.expected: All HUD buttons have aria-label or title
steps.observed: All world-viewer buttons have aria-labels: 'Select lens', 'Zoom out', 'Zoom in', 'Help', 'Close viewer', 'Reset camera', 'Toggle motion' (with aria-pressed), 'Capture Take'.
Viewfinder frame element found with class 'pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 z-10 flex items-center justify-center' and computed pointerEvents='none'. Contains AF badge and camera info.

steps.action: Identify any buttons without accessibility attributes
steps.observed: 5 buttons without aria-label/title/text found, but ALL are KanvasPage sidebar (<aside>) icon-only buttons (Image, Video, Lip Sync, Cinema Studio, Worldview icons), NOT worldview-specific elements. All worldview-specific interactive elements have proper accessibility attributes.

domInspection.sidebarButtonsNote: These are KanvasPage sidebar icon buttons, not worldview-specific

frictions.description: Store seeding required correct type structure — initial attempt with flat 'prompt' string and 'camera.transform' instead of 'cameraTransform' failed silently. The addTake action requires sceneId on the take object itself, and addWorld requires prompt as {kind:'text',text:string}.
frictions.resolution: Checked worldview.ts types and worldview-store.ts action signatures to use correct shapes

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-POLISH-006: all worldview interactive elements have proper a11y attributes, viewfinder has pointer-events-none), 1 failed (VAL-POLISH-005: horizontal overflow at narrow viewports due to .flex-1 container missing min-w-0). Takes grid correctly switches to 2-column layout below 640px.
